posts.text:
If you are counting Strait of Hormuz barrels, don’t forget to add the bypass pipelines if you want an apples-to-apples comparison to pre-war levels. 

What matters is not SoH flows, but Persian Gulf flows. As long as the bypass pipelines continue to be used (and they are), SoH
posts.summary: When assessing Persian Gulf oil exports, analysts should account for bypass pipelines alongside Strait of Hormuz flows to accurately gauge total supply levels compared to pre-war periods. The critical metric for oil markets is total Persian Gulf output rather than chokepoint transit volumes alone.

posts.text:
The Energy Department is delivering on President Trump’s Agenda to Reinvigorate the Nuclear Industrial Base:

Today, @DOE_EDF announced financing that will help rebuild America’s nuclear supply chain and accelerate the deployment of 10 new large-scale reactors. https://t.co/1IOyftvUHq
posts.summary: The Department of Energy is providing financing to rebuild the nuclear supply chain and deploy 10 new large-scale reactors, which could reduce long-term demand for fossil fuels including oil as nuclear energy expands its role in the U.S. energy mix. The initiative supports nuclear power as an alternative energy source within the Trump administration's energy policy framework.

posts.text:
Kpler's drone-based tank level data is sending a clear warning signal at cushing

The US crude market's most-watched inventory hub has shed around 11 million barrels since early May, and at the current draw pace, stocks are on course to test operational tank bottoms by mid-July. https://t.co/OHrryNIn62
posts.summary: Cushing crude inventories have declined by 11 million barrels since early May and are projected to reach operational tank bottoms by mid-July at current draw rates, signaling potential supply tightness at the US crude market's key storage hub.

posts.text: ALERT: #GasPrices appear to be rising to $3.99/gal in #Ohio today. This is a routine price cycle to an elevated amount due to the ongoing U.S./Iran situation.
posts.summary: Gas prices in Ohio are rising to approximately $3.99 per gallon due to a routine price cycle driven by tensions in the U.S./Iran situation. The alert suggests the price increase reflects broader oil market concerns related to geopolitical developments.

posts.text:
Strait of Hormuz crossings rebound

Kpler’s rolling coverage of US-Iran diplomatic developments shows a clear shift in Strait of Hormuz activity across two consecutive weekends. Between 12–14 June, total crossings reached 32. One week later, from 19–21 June, crossings rose to 93 https://t.co/L03f6wFOx1
posts.summary: Strait of Hormuz oil tanker crossings nearly tripled from 32 to 93 between mid-June and late June, suggesting improved diplomatic conditions between the US and Iran may be increasing crude flows through the critical waterway. The rebound in activity could ease supply concerns and potentially moderate oil prices if the trend continues.

The weakness has been particularly pronounced in metals linked to the energy https://t.co/kU4N2ASfA3

posts.text: The Hormuz crisis is triggering a global race to build oil reserves. From India and Australia to Singapore and Pakistan, governments are expanding storage capacity to boost energy security, potentially creating demand for up to 1 billion barrels over the coming years. #Oil
posts.summary: Governments across Asia and the Indo-Pacific are rapidly expanding oil storage capacity in response to Hormuz security concerns, with potential demand for up to 1 billion barrels over the coming years. This strategic stockpiling initiative could significantly support global oil demand and prices amid heightened geopolitical risks to a critical shipping chokepoint.

posts.text:
Iran Rushes to Woo Asia’s Largest Oil Importers After US Waiver

Sellers including middlemen and representatives from the National Iranian Oil Co. made contact with refiners in India, Japan, South Korea and elsewhere even before the license was officially granted, according to
posts.summary: Iran is actively reaching out to major Asian oil refiners in India, Japan, and South Korea following a U.S. waiver, with Iranian oil sellers and representatives initiating contact even before the license was officially approved. This suggests Iran is moving quickly to secure buyers and potentially increase oil exports to key Asian markets.

posts.text: The Strait of Hormuz may be reopening, but tanker markets remain in crisis mode. Daily tanker rates have nearly doubled to over $190,000, while some VLCCs are reportedly earning up to $470,000 per day as vessel shortages persist. #Oil #Tankers #Hormuz #Shipping
posts.summary: Tanker rates have surged significantly with daily costs exceeding $190,000 and some VLCCs earning up to $470,000 per day due to vessel shortages, indicating tight shipping capacity despite the potential reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. These elevated transportation costs could add pressure to crude oil prices and upstream economics in the near term.

posts.text:
Global oil trade, including oil, condensate and refined products, is expected to rise by nearly 25% by 2050. 

Interregional crude and condensate trade stood at around 37.3 mb/d in 2025. By 2030, it is expected to increase to 41.8 mb/d, supported by rising oil demand in major https://t.co/qjExFbxenp
posts.summary: OPEC forecasts global oil trade to grow nearly 25% by 2050, with interregional crude and condensate trade expanding from 37.3 mb/d in 2025 to 41.8 mb/d by 2030, driven by rising oil demand in major markets.

posts.text:
Approvals for new gas-fired power plants rose to a 25-year high last year

Until 2023, most gas-fired power plant approvals were in gas-importing countries, but the US now dominates orders, driven by data centre demand

Learn more in our report ➡️ https://t.co/8IajZj1gIL https://t.co/DBX0xeI2as
posts.summary: Gas-fired power plant approvals reached a 25-year high in 2023, with the US leading approvals due to data center demand, signaling sustained natural gas demand in the near term. This shift in approvals toward gas infrastructure in the US could support energy sector investments and influence commodity pricing, though the direct oil market implications are not specified in this statement.

posts.text:

Generational blending economics and global energy security concerns create fresh demand opportunities for US ethanol.

posts.text: Keep an eye on European refiners this week. They are NOT configured to operate when ambient temperatures are above 100 degrees Fahrenheit.  Lower yields are likely across the Continent.

This supply disruption could tighten crude oil processing capacity and potentially support energy prices in Europe.

posts.text:
China to keep clean oil product export curbs in July; policy may shift in Aug

🔷 US-Iran progress may encourage Beijing to relax controls
🔷 Gasoil exports to Australia resume in May
🔷 Ample export quotas may boost outflow in H2

posts.summary: China is maintaining restrictions on clean oil product exports through July but may ease controls in August, potentially influenced by US-Iran diplomatic developments. The resumption of gasoil exports to Australia and ample export quotas could increase Chinese oil product outflows in the second half of the year.

posts.text:
European gas markets don't move in isolation. Storage dynamics, shifting LNG flows, pipeline policy, geopolitical risk. It all connects, and it all moves fast.

EA Live Europe Gas brings you real-time updates and expert analysis from our global team, tracking how worldwide https://t.co/6LVy7dp4OY
posts.summary: Energy Aspects highlights the interconnected nature of European gas markets, which are influenced by storage levels, LNG flows, pipeline policy, and geopolitical factors—dynamics that can have ripple effects across broader energy markets including oil. The post emphasizes the need for real-time monitoring of these rapidly evolving market conditions through their coverage service.

posts.text: Interesting analysis from Goldman on EVs. They estimate total hit to global oil demand of 130,000-320,000 b/d by end-2027 despite accelerated sales that have pushed EV share of vehicle market up 3.4% last month to 26.1% (all-time high).
posts.summary: Goldman Sachs estimates that accelerating EV adoption will reduce global oil demand by 130,000-320,000 barrels per day by the end of 2027, even as electric vehicles reach an all-time market share of 26.1%. The analysis suggests EVs' impact on oil consumption remains modest relative to overall demand despite record sales growth.

posts.text:
We have more insight than ever. So why are decisions getting harder? 

Markets are more volatile. Decisions more complex. The cost of delay is higher than ever. 

The challenge isn’t knowing more. It’s acting, quickly and with clarity. 

CERA Titan: https://t.co/vhIqgYP6tU https://t.co/lt4iZvjVMw
posts.summary: S&P Global Energy highlights that despite increased data availability, energy market participants face growing complexity and volatility that demand faster decision-making to avoid costly delays. The post suggests that quick, decisive action rather than additional information is becoming the critical differentiator in today's energy markets.

posts.text:
Stay ahead of #HurricaneSeason with our revamped Interactive U.S. Gulf Coast Refinery & Hurricane Path Map! Track storms, receive instant alerts, and safeguard your supply chain from #GulfCoast disruptions. Register for free access: https://t.co/ykpjmZmczV

posts.summary: OPIS has launched an updated interactive map tool that allows users to track hurricane paths and monitor U.S. Gulf Coast refineries, helping oil market participants mitigate supply chain disruptions during hurricane season. The free tool provides real-time alerts to help stakeholders protect refining operations from weather-related impacts in a region critical to U.S. petroleum supply.
